items.title: Early gigantic lamniform marks the onset of mega-body size in modern shark evolution
items.author: Benjamin P. Kear
items.content: The earliest appearance of mega-body size in sharks is pushed back by 15 million years with the discovery of new fossils from Northern Australia. Using a comprehensive dataset of living sharks to estimate sizes of extinct taxa, results demonstrate that mega-body size is an ancient trait.

items.title: The gut feeling in motion sickness
items.author: Ksander N. de Winkel
items.content: Motion sickness, a common response to unnatural motion, varies widely among individuals. A review of recent findings highlights the gut’s active role, including its brain-body pathways potentially driving pathophysiology and hinting at a more complex model than previously thought.

items.title: An uncharacterized small protein MicN mediates the transcriptional reprogramming of Salmonella through regulating the RpoS-RNA polymerase interaction
items.author: Bingqing Li
items.content: The small protein MicN functions as an anti-sigma factor of RpoS, playing a crucial role in Salmonella survival within macrophages by modulating bacterial metabolism.

items.title: Comparative venomics suggests an evolutionary adaption of spider venom from predation to defense
items.author: Andreas Vilcinskas
items.content: The spider Cheiracanthium punctorium evolved defensive venom rich in CSTX peptides and PLA2 enzymes. These components arose via gene duplication, fusion, and convergence, reflecting how similar ecological pressures shape venom evolution.

items.title: Sterols are key to coral larvae survival, swimming capacity, and thermal tolerance
items.author: David J. Suggett
items.content: Targeted lipid nanoparticle supplementation in Acropora spathulata enhances larval swimming, settlement, and juvenile resilience, revealing critical roles of sterols and fatty acids in early life-stage fitness of corals.

items.title: Bacillus subtilis TO-A suppresses the age-related decline in locomotion in Caenorhabditis elegans
items.author: Satoshi Shimazaki
items.content: Aged C. elegans fed Bacillus subtilis TO-A (BSTOA) maintain mobility and actin integrity and exhibit increased resistance to oxidative stress. These findings implicate BSTOA-induced maintenance of gpx-7 expression as a key mechanism.

items.title: Quantitative proteome of bacterial periplasmic predation by Bdellovibrio bacteriovorus reveals a prey-lytic protease
items.author: Simona G. Huwiler
items.content: The quantitative proteome of Bdellovibrio bacteriovorus during its periplasmic predation on Escherichia coli detected a prey-lytic protease. This proteome is valuable to study this bacterial interaction and find novel antimicrobial enzymes.

items.title: Iron overload in CD11c<sup>+</sup> myeloid cells exacerbates acetaminophen hepatotoxicity
items.author: Toshiro Moroishi
items.content: FBXL5 deficiency in CD11c+ myeloid cells causes iron overload, enhancing NF-κB recruitment to the IL-6 promoter and increasing IL-6 production. This exacerbates neutrophil infiltration and liver injury in mice, while IL-6 neutralization mitigates damage, revealing iron regulation as a therapeutic target.
